biography: Riku, notably recognized as the brother of fellow competitor Panu, marked his sole contribution to the Hector Trophée's history during one of its esteemed past editions. His participation, a singular event, served as a distinctive chapter, linking the familial legacy of the brothers within this unique pair competition.

During his solitary appearance, Riku engaged with the demanding formats of the tournament, striving within both the individual and pair play. While his efforts did not result in claiming the coveted Hector Trophée with a partner or securing the Victor Trophy as the individual champion, his presence on the greens added to the competitive fabric of that particular year's event.

As the Hector Trophée 2026 approaches, Riku is recognized as having concluded his active involvement with the tournament, and he will not be among the competitors. His unique and brief journey, etched into the tournament's narrative alongside his brother Panu, signifies a valued, albeit concise, part of the Hector Trophée's developing heritage.
